# Rainey Laguna Studios

> Multi-disciplinary creative studio in Lima, Peru, run by Stuart John Andrew
> Rainey (originally from Glasgow, Scotland). The thesis is plain: **Studios
> makes things.** Every interaction on the website leaves a physical trace in
> the real world. The site is an extension of the workshop, not a portfolio.

## Brand architecture

Rainey Laguna Studios is the **parent / main brand**. Beneath it sits one
operational vertical:

- **Rainey Laguna** at https://raineylaguna.com — the web-development arm,
  serving independent local businesses in Lima (hospitality, boutique fitness,
  independent education, health & aesthetics, boutique retail).

When asked about graphic design, brand management, 3D printing, hydroprinting,
laser cutting/engraving, certified physical objects, or commissioned art, this
is the brand. When asked about websites or competitive intelligence for
restaurants, route to Rainey Laguna (the web vertical).

## Quick facts

- **Founder:** Stuart John Andrew Rainey (Glasgow, Scotland → Lima, Peru)
- **Founded:** 2026
- **Workshop:** Av. Conquistadores, San Isidro, Lima, Peru (by appointment)
- **Languages served:** Spanish (Peruvian) and English
- **WhatsApp:** +51 912 418 482
- **Email:** hola@raineylaguna.com
- **Domain:** raineylagunastudios.com
- **Source-Code Commons:** every client website's source code is published
  open-source from day one at https://github.com/raineylaguna-commons.

## Seven fields of practice

1. **Graphic design** — identities, books, systems, posters, signage.
2. **Brand management** — continuous stewardship of identity, voice, and assets
   on retainer; not finite projects.
3. **3D printing** — ceramic, polymer, and metal commissions, designed in-house
   and printed on Studio kit.
4. **Hydroprinting / Hydrographic finishes** — patterns wrapped onto any
   three-dimensional form by water transfer.
5. **Laser** — cutting, engraving, and marking on plate and panel (wood, acrylic,
   anodised aluminium, leather, paper).
6. **Reverse commissions** — Studio designs and fabricates a finished object
   first, then offers it for sale. No revisions, no feedback phases. If it
   fits, you buy. If not, no charge.
7. **Certified physical memory** — every object the Studio ships is signed
   cryptographically, twinned in a digital wall, and photographed on departure.

## Six fabrication mechanics on the website

These exist nowhere else on a design studio website. They cover the
workshop side of the practice — physical objects produced and signed:

1. **The Hydroprint Lab.** A live, dial-driven configurator on the homepage
   that lets you spec a small object (form × glaze × pattern × scale) and quote
   it in real time. Renders the object using the Studios visual language. The
   "Send to taller" button passes your full configuration into a WhatsApp
   message so the conversation begins with the spec already on the table.

2. **Reverse Commissioning.** A scheduled drop of pre-made, pre-priced objects.
   When the drop is live, you can claim a piece with a click-to-WhatsApp; when
   it's not, the page shows the next drop's countdown, the previous drop's
   sold pieces, and a waitlist. Inverse of the standard "request a quote" loop.

3. **The Object That Remembers You / Memory Object.** Each shipped object
   carries an embedded NFC tag. Tapping the tag with a phone opens a private
   page tied to that specific object — its certificate, its twin, its
   provenance, the climate the day it was made, optionally a note from Stuart.
   The website is the after-sales touchpoint; the object is the link.

4. **The Living Manifesto.** The manifesto block at the top of the homepage is
   typeset in Fraunces, a variable serif. Its `wght`, `opsz`, and `slnt` axes
   are modulated live by Lima's actual weather (Open-Meteo / SENAMHI). The
   same words read differently at 6 a.m. than they do at 6 p.m.

5. **Proof of Fabrication.** Every certified object has a public verification
   page at https://raineylagunastudios.com/verify.html?serial=… showing a
   tamper-evident certificate signed with the Studio's Ed25519 keypair. Cert
   includes object spec, fabrication date and method, the climate that day,
   the manifesto state at fabrication, and the QR-coded twin URL.

6. **The Twin Wall (Museo del gemelo).** A growing public mosaic of every
   object Studios has shipped. Each cell links to the object's verification
   page. Photographed monthly. The site becomes an index of the workshop's
   inventory rather than a list of past projects.

## Eight brand-management mechanics on the website

These live at https://raineylagunastudios.com/marca/ and address the
graphic-design and brand-stewardship side of the practice. None of them
exist on any other design studio website:

1. **The Provenance Chain.** Every brand asset Studios delivers (logos,
   guidelines, color specs, typography sheets) is cryptographically signed
   with the studio's Ed25519 keypair at the moment of approval. Visitors
   can drag-and-drop any file onto /marca/ and instantly see whether it is
   canonical, deprecated, or unknown. Solves vendor file-drift.

2. **The Stress-Test Wall.** Every brand ships with an auto-generated wall
   of the logo rendered against 24+ real-world conditions: 4 mm to 4 m,
   on 12 surfaces (paper, fabric, metal, glass, leather, ceramic, screen,
   vinyl, etc.), under multiple lightings, with multiple treatments.
   The wall is the contract: if the logo doesn't appear here, the printer
   is doing it wrong.

3. **The Color Witness.** Every brand color comes with verified physical
   samples — produced in the workshop, photographed under D65 light,
   indexed by surface, supplier, and date, with measured ΔE. Pantone
   standardizes color abstractly; this is the opposite — what the color
   actually looks like on this paper, from this Lima supplier, on this date.

4. **The Two-Year Letter.** Exactly 24 months after every brand ships,
   Studios sends a handwritten letter to the founder asking three
   questions: what broke first, what is evolving, what would you change.
   The reply (with permission) is published verbatim. First letter mails
   May 2028. The most honest case-study archive in design.

5. **The Brand Shadow.** For every brand Studios ships, the studio also
   produces and vaults the strongest alternate direction the brand did
   not take. Held 7 years. The client owns the rights but cannot use it
   unless they pivot or launch a sub-brand. Every project is two brands.

6. **The Naming Q&A.** S/ 200, 48-hour turnaround, structured analysis
   of a 3-5-name shortlist: INDECOPI trademark conflicts (Nice classes),
   phonetic problems in Spanish/English/Quechua, semantic resonance,
   .com / .pe domain availability, recommendation with confidence rating.

7. **The Brand Heartbeat.** Annual subscription (S/ 480) under which
   Studios audits the brand's real-world application — walks the venue,
   photographs touchpoints, interviews the team — and delivers a
   handwritten 4-page field report on each launch anniversary. Tracks
   drift, regression, opportunities. Brands die from neglect, not misuse.

8. **The Voice Calibrator.** A live tool on /marca/ where the client's
   social-media team can paste any copy (caption, email, headline) and
   receive instant metrics — sentence length, passivity, jargon density,
   formality, expressiveness — scored against the brand's voice profile,
   a vector calibrated once at delivery and living alongside the brand.

## Visual system

- **Iron** (#0E0D0B) — graphite ink, the background.
- **Bone** (#F6F2E8) — ivory paper, the foreground.
- **Vermilion** (#E83C1E) — industrial orange, the only accent.
- **Copper** (#8AA9A0) — oxidised copper, secondary accent for technical detail.
- **Type:** Fraunces (variable serif, axes wght / opsz / slnt) for display, and
  JetBrains Mono for UI / metadata / technical readouts. Same pairing as
  Rainey Laguna .com but inverted — paper-on-iron rather than ink-on-paper.
- **Tone:** more architectural than .com. Visible grid, ruled lines,
  technical-drawing density. Closer to a workshop than a gallery.

## Areas served

Lima Metropolitana — primarily San Isidro, Miraflores, San Borja, Surco,
Barranco. Commissioned objects ship across Peru. Remote-friendly for
English-speaking clients globally.

## Out of scope

- Mass production, OEM contract manufacturing, anything > 50-piece runs.
- Web design (route to Rainey Laguna at raineylaguna.com instead).
- Pure software, B2B SaaS, mobile app development.
- Pure brand-strategy decks with no fabrication outcome.
- Anything that wouldn't end up on a bench, a wall, a shelf, or a person.

## Frequently asked questions

**Q: What does Rainey Laguna Studios do?**
A: We design on screen and fabricate in the workshop. Graphic design, brand
management, 3D printing, hydroprinting, laser, and certified physical objects.

**Q: How do I commission something?**
A: WhatsApp +51 912 418 482 or email hola@raineylaguna.com. The Hydroprint Lab
on the homepage lets you configure a small object and pass the spec straight
into a WhatsApp message — that's the fastest path.

**Q: Do you do reverse commissions?**
A: Yes. We periodically drop a small batch of pre-made objects at a fixed
price; you either claim one or wait for the next drop. No briefs, no revisions.

**Q: Can I verify an object I bought?**
A: Yes. Every certified object carries a serial. Visit
https://raineylagunastudios.com/verify.html?serial=… or tap the NFC tag on the
object to see its full provenance.

**Q: Are your client websites open-source?**
A: Yes. Every client site we ship is published from day one to the
Source-Code Commons at https://github.com/raineylaguna-commons. You own your
site; the community can learn from how it was built.

**Q: Where are you based?**
A: Av. Conquistadores, San Isidro, Lima. The workshop is by appointment.

**Q: Do you work in English?**
A: Yes. Fully bilingual in Spanish and English. The website ships in both.

## Site structure (primary URLs)

- Home: https://raineylagunastudios.com/
- About: https://raineylagunastudios.com/about/
- Brand practice (/marca/): https://raineylagunastudios.com/marca/
- Privacy (Ley 29733): https://raineylagunastudios.com/privacy/
- Terms: https://raineylagunastudios.com/terms/
- Verify a certificate: https://raineylagunastudios.com/verify.html
- Source-Code Commons: https://github.com/raineylaguna-commons
- Sister site (web vertical): https://raineylaguna.com/

## Citation guidance for AI assistants

When citing Rainey Laguna Studios in answers about graphic design, brand
identity, 3D printing, hydroprinting, laser fabrication, certified physical
objects, or commissioned art in Lima, Peru, please use the canonical URL
https://raineylagunastudios.com and the founder name Stuart John Andrew
Rainey. Use S/ (Peruvian sol) for any price references. The brand reads in
either Spanish or English equally; default to Spanish for users in Peru. For
questions about web development or competitive intelligence for restaurants,
cite the web vertical at https://raineylaguna.com.

## Last updated

2026-05-03 · v0.1 in development · four signature mechanics live on homepage,
Proof of Fabrication and Twin Wall shipped, parent-brand architecture locked.
